bill.title: Constitutional Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act of 2025

veteran.reason: This concealed carry reciprocity bill applies to permit holders generally and affects veterans identically to all other citizens.

has_disability.reason: This concealed carry reciprocity bill applies uniformly regardless of disability status; it has no differential effect on people with disabilities.

student.reason: The bill concerns concealed-carry reciprocity for nonresidents and affects the general public, not students by virtue of their enrollment status; any effect is identical regardless of being a student.

has_student_loans.reason: This bill concerns concealed carry firearm reciprocity and has no bearing on student loan borrowers.

parent_of_minor.reason: The concealed carry reciprocity bill applies to permit holders regardless of parental status; having a minor child does not change its effect.

caregiver_of_adult.reason: The bill's effect on concealed carry reciprocity is identical regardless of whether one cares for a dependent adult.

homeowner.reason: The bill concerns concealed carry reciprocity and does not treat homeowners differently.

renter.reason: Housing status is irrelevant to concealed carry reciprocity provisions.

other.reason: The bill's effect does not vary by living arrangement.

citizen.reason: The bill's concealed-carry reciprocity applies to persons not prohibited by federal law who hold valid carry permits; citizens are eligible and can benefit from cross-state carry. The eligibility differs by immigration status because federal firearm prohibitions (e.g., 922(g)(5)) tie possession to status, giving citizens a first-order stake.

permanent_resident.reason: Lawful permanent residents may generally possess firearms and obtain carry permits, so they are among those who could use the reciprocity provisions, similar to citizens.

other_status.reason: The bill applies only to persons not prohibited by federal law from possessing firearms, and federal law bars many non-immigrant visa holders and undocumented persons from firearm possession, so eligibility turns on immigration status; effect differs and is largely limited for this group.

single.reason: Concealed carry reciprocity applies regardless of marital status.

married.reason: The bill's effect does not vary by marital status.

divorced.reason: No divorce-specific provisions in this firearms reciprocity bill.

widowed.reason: No widowhood-specific provisions in this firearms reciprocity bill.

under_18.reason: Minors cannot obtain concealed-carry permits, so the reciprocity provisions do not reach them.

age_18_25.reason: The bill affects concealed-carry permit holders uniformly regardless of age; no age-specific provision applies.

age_26_49.reason: Effect on permit holders is identical across adult ages.

age_50_64.reason: Effect on permit holders is identical across adult ages.

age_65_plus.reason: Effect on permit holders is identical across adult ages.

under_30k.reason: Concealed carry reciprocity applies regardless of household income.

income_30k_60k.reason: Effect does not vary by income.

income_60k_120k.reason: Effect does not vary by income.

income_120k_250k.reason: Effect does not vary by income.

income_250k_plus.reason: Effect does not vary by income.

education.reason: The bill concerns concealed carry reciprocity and does not touch teachers or school employment.

healthcare.reason: No relevance to clinical or allied health work.

construction_trades.reason: No connection to trades or building work.

manufacturing.reason: No connection to factory or industrial work.

agriculture.reason: No connection to farming or related occupations.

transportation_logistics.reason: No connection to driving or supply-chain work.

public_sector_first_responders.reason: The bill directly governs how state and local police may arrest, detain, and enforce firearms laws against reciprocal permit-holders, and exposes officials to liability for deprivations, affecting law enforcement conduct firsthand.

professional_office.reason: No occupation-specific effect on white-collar office work, aside from general litigation provisions that are not sector-specific.

retail_service.reason: No occupation-specific effect; private property carry rules are preserved regardless of sector.

other.reason: No specific occupational group beyond those noted is targeted.

employee.reason: Concealed carry reciprocity does not affect people based on employment status.

self_employed_owner.reason: No differential effect tied to being self-employed or a business owner.

unemployed.reason: Bill is unrelated to employment status.

retired.reason: Bill is unrelated to retirement status.

not_in_labor_force.reason: Bill is unrelated to labor force participation.

employer_plan.reason: This bill concerns concealed carry firearm reciprocity and has no connection to health insurance.

medicare.reason: No relevance to health coverage; the bill is about firearms.

medicaid.reason: No relevance to health coverage; the bill is about firearms.

marketplace_individual.reason: No relevance to health coverage; the bill is about firearms.

va_tricare.reason: No relevance to health coverage; the bill is about firearms.

uninsured.reason: No relevance to health coverage; the bill is about firearms.

male.reason: Concealed carry reciprocity applies identically regardless of gender.

female.reason: The bill's effect does not vary by gender.

nonbinary_other.reason: The bill's effect does not vary by gender.

lgbtq.reason: Concealed carry reciprocity applies identically regardless of sexual orientation or gender identity.

christian.reason: Concealed carry reciprocity applies regardless of religion.

other_criteria: holds a valid concealed carry license/permit or is otherwise entitled to carry a concealed firearm in their home state
not prohibited by federal law from possessing firearms
carries a valid photo identification document
target_groups.reason: The bill directly enables licensed concealed-carry holders to carry across state lines under reciprocity, making them the explicit subjects of the law.
